prevalence.source: Ministério da Saúde / SBP + WHO diarrhea control context
prevalence.trend: decreasing severe outcomes with ORS and vaccination; outbreaks occur

burden.dalyPerCapita: high per-case in dehydration; overall decreasing mortality
burden.mortalityRate: Low compared to historical levels; concentrated in vulnerable groups

vulnerablePopulations: children in areas with poor sanitation
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis based on stool frequency/consistency and dehydration assessment; stool testing reserved for severe/persistent/bloody diarrhea; WHO dehydration classification

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with timely ORS/zinc and dehydration management

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS focuses on dehydration assessment and danger signs; lab testing limited in primary care
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: ORS + zinc, continued feeding/breastfeeding; IV fluids for severe dehydration; antibiotics only for specific indications (e.g., dysentery)
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Sanitation disparities and seasonal outbreaks; risk of inappropriate antibiotic use
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: SUS access to primary care, ORS distribution, and rotavirus vaccination program
